rulings.Case Title: DONALD ROUTT VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 14, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Certainteed Corporation's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is continued on the court's own motion to October 23, 2014. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY CHARLIFUE et al VS. ALLIED PACKING & SUPPLY, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 14, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Goodman Lumber Company's motion for summary judgment: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS BELL VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: First Appearance Fee Paid On: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 14, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant FDCC California, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiff's complaint is denied. The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support a claim for punitive damages and defendant has withdrawn its argument that such a claim is barred against a dissolved corporation. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
